nodes.name: Config – GHL IDs
nodes.notes: Same Retail Sales / Visualizer-Estimate-Submitted IDs as Workflow 1.

nodes.name: Build Contact Payload (Estimate Scoped, Filter Nulls)
nodes.notes: SCOPED WRITE FIX: allow-list only includes firstName/lastName/email/phone/address1 + lead_source/property_address/estimated_roof_size/estimate_range. current_roof_type, selected_roof_type, project_reason, insurance_claim_status, homeowner_timeline are never read from the payload — structurally impossible to write, even as null. Uses named node selectors per Workflow 1 lessons learned, never $input.first().json.

nodes.name: Search Opportunities For Contact
nodes.notes: VERIFY before activating: query param casing for /opportunities/search is unconfirmed in this session (legacy GHL endpoints sometimes use snake_case instead of the camelCase used elsewhere in v2). Check the real GHL docs / a test call and adjust location_id/pipeline_id/contact_id below if they don't match.

nodes.name: Check Existing Opportunity
nodes.notes: Dedup + stage-guard logic: if an opportunity already exists for this contact, never create a duplicate. Only re-affirm the stage if it's still sitting at Visualizer/Estimate Submitted (stage 1) — if sales has already moved it forward (Contacted, Appointment Set, etc.), leave it alone so an estimate-tool resubmission can't knock it backward.

nodes.name: Execute Workflow – New Lead Alert SMS
nodes.notes: Workflow 5 not built yet — continueOnFail keeps estimate-tool lead intake working regardless. Set the real workflowId once Workflow 5 exists. Pulls contactId/firstName/phone/source from Check Existing Opportunity, not $json, since $json varies depending on which branch (create/updateStage/noop) executed.
